hypotheses.name: SV4 confidence quintile gradient
hypotheses.method: Split 3092 STRING pairs (score>=0.4) into 5 quintiles by score. For each quintile compute mean SV4 co-pole z-score (K=52) across all 12 layers. Report Spearman rho of quintile midpoint vs mean z.
hypotheses.primary_metric: Spearman rho (quintile midpoint vs mean z-score)
hypotheses.next_action: Include in paper as third axis replication of the monotonic confidence gradient (SV2 rho=1.00, SV3 rho=0.90, SV4 rho=0.90)

hypotheses.name: Axis-dominant pair GO composition (biological orthogonality)
hypotheses.method: Assign each STRING pair a dominant axis (SV2/SV3/SV4) based on which axis shows most co-pole hits across layers 7,8,11. Extract gene sets per axis. Run Fisher's exact GO enrichment (591 terms, size 3-50) per gene set. Compute GO Jaccard of top-20 hits between axis pairs.
hypotheses.primary_metric: GO Jaccard of top-20 enriched terms between axis-dominant gene sets
hypotheses.next_action: Use as primary evidence for biological orthogonality in paper Methods/Results. SV2=innate immunity/exosome, SV3=membrane/cell-surface, SV4=receptor-binding/apoptosis.

hypotheses.name: TRRUST signed regulation in SV3/SV4
hypotheses.method: TRRUST activation (N=116) and repression (N=64) pairs. For SV3 and SV4 at each layer: compare absolute projection distance (|tf_proj - target_proj|) between activation and repression pairs using MWU test. Activation expected to co-pole (smaller distance).
hypotheses.next_action: Retire TRRUST signed regulation on SV3/SV4. SV2 retains the signed regulation signal (12/12 layers). Document as axis-specific property.
hypotheses.retirement_reason: 2 negative tests across SV3 and SV4; SV2 was the only axis with signed regulation signal
